% mckey-shuffle.tex -- answer-key + option-pinning checks UNDER \shuffle, with a
% pinned seed so the layout is reproducible.  The test's correctness assertions
% are seed-INVARIANT (they read the actual rendered slot of each \cchoice needle
% and compare it to the key letter), so they hold on any platform regardless of
% where the shuffle lands the options; the pin just makes a failure reproducible.
\documentclass{autoexam}
\versions{A}
\setexamseed{4242}
\shuffle
\solutions
\examsetup{number=1, date={Engine Test}}
\loadbank{mckey-bank.tex}

\begin{document}
\begin{mcproblems}
	\problem{mc_shuf}
	\problem{mc_fixed}
	\problem{mc_force}
	\problem{mc_choose}
\end{mcproblems}
\end{document}
